refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain analysis, avoiding any implementation details and maintaining solution neutrality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an analysis of the terrain features of the worksite independently of other site features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Extract and classify terrain features relevant to safety and operations
Provide an interface to share the terrain model with planning and execution modules
Continuously update the terrain model based on new data inputs
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a comprehensive terrain model as a standalone deliverable, which can be independently developed and then shared with other functions. This supports independence and clear boundaries.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of sufficient data to generate an accurate terrain model
Risk that terrain features critical to safety may be omitted if not properly defined
Assumes that planning and execution functions can consume the terrain model in the provided format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of 'key site features' to ensure all safety-critical and operationally relevant terrain aspects are included in the model.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for processing and generating digital terrain models and site feature representations

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data and site measurements for terrain modeling

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands to initiate terrain model generation

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends generated digital terrain models and site feature data to other system components

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain modeling is core to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as data inputs and outputs are well encapsulated.
Low containment; vehicle mainly for mobility, not terrain modeling; would require extensive diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not data processing; poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services unrelated to terrain modeling; very poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the digital terrain model generation DP because it specializes in processing site data and generating models, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may affect model accuracy.
If terrain model data is needed by multiple modules, interface management must ensure minimal coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by specifying the creation of a digital terrain model that captures key site features, which aligns with the requirement for terrain analysis.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may affect model accuracy.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and containment score (9) for the DP, with minimal diffusion (score 2). The DP is well encapsulated within this module, and external interfaces are clearly defined for input (Telemetry, Tasking) and output (Network Link). Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives raw sensor data and commands, processes them, and outputs the digital terrain model to other system components, enabling traceability and validation against acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and justified by the module's specialization in terrain modeling and the containment hypothesis, ensuring ownership and responsibility are well defined.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage the dependency on telemetry data quality and timeliness to mitigate risks to model accuracy.
Ensure interface management is robust to minimize coupling when terrain model data is consumed by multiple modules.
Consider documenting explicit data validation and error handling strategies within the NGHE Solution to address potential input data issues.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justification and strong containment of the DP within the NGHE Solution IM. The DP directly satisfies the FR, and the IM boundary is well defined with high cohesion and minimal diffusion. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although there are some risks related to telemetry data dependency and interface management, these are manageable through monitoring and documentation rather than requiring further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain a clear and stable architecture before proceeding to further sub-FR derivation.

revise.why: To incorporate explicit data validation and error handling strategies within the NGHE Solution module to mitigate risks from telemetry data quality and timeliness.

assumptions: Telemetry data input quality and timeliness can be monitored and managed effectively.
The NGHE Solution module has sufficient capability to handle data validation and error handling internally.
open_risks: Dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input affecting model accuracy.
Potential coupling issues when terrain model data is consumed by multiple modules.
evidence_needed: Verification results demonstrating the NGHE Solution module's ability to produce accurate digital terrain models under varying telemetry data conditions.
Documentation of interface management and error handling strategies within the NGHE Solution module.
